recalls.Description: CCCEI-branded power strips with 6ft, 10ft, and 15ft cords have been recalled due to a serious fire hazard. These power strips feature a black metal casing, six black plastic receptacles, and individual on/off switches for each outlet. A warning label on the back advises users to keep children away and to use the strips only indoors in dry locations to minimize the risk of electric shock.
recalls.Title: CCCEI Power Strips Recalled Due to Fire Hazard Risk; Available from Middle Way Electronics
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Middle Way Electronics via email at CCCEIpowerstrips@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: Dresbe is recalling its LED Christmas Headbands due to a serious safety risk. These headbands, sold in a two-pack featuring a red candy cane design and a green Christmas tree design, contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. This poses a significant risk of ingestion, which can lead to severe injuries or even fatalities. The headbands are labeled with "HB-052" and "DRESBE LED Christmas Headband (2pc)" on their packaging.
recalls.Title: Dresbe LED Christmas Headbands Recalled Due to Ingestion Hazard from Button Cell Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Dresbe via email at dresberecall@shineprom.com.

Distributors.Name: Wenxi Xuefeng Technology Co., Ltd, dba Dresbe, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries, violating safety standards.

recalls.Description: Kitosun is recalling its Submersible LED Lights due to a potential hazard posed by accessible lithium coin batteries. These lights come with 10 color-changing LEDs, two remote controls, and a total of 20 preinstalled CR2450 batteries in the lights and four CR2032 batteries in the remotes. Each light measures approximately 3.5 cm in diameter. It's important to note that button cell and coin batteries can be dangerous, and proper disposal or recycling should follow local hazardous waste guidelines.
recalls.Title: Kitosun Submersible LED Lights Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risk from Accessible Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Kitosun via email at sales@kitosun.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kitosun Submersible LED Lights

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2024 through November 2025 for approximately $23.

Distributors.Name: Kitosun Original Company Limited, dba Kitosun, of China

Hazards.Name: The recalled LED lights do not comply with safety standards as they allow easy access to lithium coin batteries, posing a serious ingestion risk to children. Ingesting these batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should ensure the recalled lights are kept away from children and contact Kitosun to obtain a full refund or a complimentary replacement. A photo of the product in the trash will be required to process the refund or replacement, which should be emailed to gumilius@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Kitosun recalls Submersible LED Lights due to battery ingestion risks. Contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Energizer is recalling its Large Power Indicator Lantern due to a potential overheating issue that can pose a burn risk to users. This portable lantern features a cone shape with an opaque lens and displays the "Energizer" logo on its side. The top section is gray, equipped with a prominent power button and a red handle. The model number is BGAAL9, and the SKU can be found as either 039800143341 or 039800143334, located on the packaging. Only lanterns marked with a date code of June 2024 on the base are included in this recall. The date code is represented by two circles, indicating the month and year.

recalls.Description: The Isla Rae brand's magnetic wireless chargers have been recalled due to serious fire and burn hazards. These chargers, designed to attach magnetically to the back of compatible phones, were sold in white, pink, and purple colors. The model number 'RM5PBM' is located on the side of the charger, beneath the label '5000 mAh 3.7V.' It is crucial to handle the recalled lithium-ion batteries properly, as they pose a higher risk of fire compared to other batteries. Do not dispose of them in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.

Only units with serial numbers 000G21, 000H21, 000I21, and 000L21 are affected by this recall. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly, as they pose a higher fire risk compared to standard batteries. Only units with serial numbers beginning with 'M2' are affected by this recall, which can be found etched on the bottom of the device. The UPC code for the product packaging is 628078800836.

These color-changing, dimmable lights, measuring approximately 0.4 inches, were available in various colors including white, warm white, blue, ice blue, green, red, yellow, purple, and pink. The design of these lights allows easy access to the lithium coin batteries, which poses a serious safety threat.
recalls.Title: AliExpress Recalls RGB LED Strip Lights Due to Serious Ingestion Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should visit AliExpress's online service at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/home?language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp, email us_product_recall@aliexpress.com, or access more details at https://helpcenter.aliexpress.com/s/BuyerHelp/knowledge?categoryId=1000110454&language=en_US&m_station=BuyerHelp&questionId=1061297473&regulation=US-product-recall. Additional information can be found at www.aliexpress.com by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Casely is recalling its Power Pods 5000mAh portable MagSafe wireless phone chargers, identified by model number E33A. They were available in various colors and prints and weigh approximately 3.2 ounces. It's crucial to handle these lithium-ion batteries correctly, as improper disposal can increase fire risks. Consumers should not discard them in regular trash or recycling streams. Instead, check with local hazardous waste collection centers for proper disposal options.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has announced a recall of its BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers, which feature a plastic body and an LCD display. These devices are gray with a blue top and prominently display the Black Diamond logo along with the model name 'RECON LT' on the front. The affected transceivers were available for purchase individually and as part of Black Diamond’s Alpine Avy Safety Sets and Recon LT Avy Safety Sets.

recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled avalanche transceivers and reach out to Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email 2025reconltrecall@bdel.com. Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the switch mechanism malfunctioning due to corrosion. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), and various specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from May 2021 through February 2025, priced between $350 and $500.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Corrosion of the metal contact in the switch mechanism can lead to device malfunction, potentially preventing the transceiver from functioning during an avalanche, posing a serious risk of injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled transceivers and contact Black Diamond for a full refund, replacement, or free repair. To participate in the recall, visit https://warranty.bdel.com/beaconRecall2025/StepOne. Repairs will replace the original zinc-coated steel insert with a corrosion-resistant stainless-steel component. Consumers can also opt to exchange their BD Recon LT for a BD Guide BT or BD Recon X transceiver, which feature a different switch design, or receive a full refund.

recalls.Description: The Glow in Dark Party Supplies Toy Sets, which include various glowing items and bunny ear headbands with button cell batteries, are being recalled due to safety concerns. The sets feature 250 glow sticks, 12 foam glow sticks, 12 LED glasses, and 12 bunny ear headbands in assorted colors. The design allows easy access to the batteries, posing a serious ingestion hazard for children. Swallowing these batteries can lead to severe injuries, including internal chemical burns and even death.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Dynabook at 800-457-7777 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or email dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com. More information is available online at https://support.dynabook.com/recall2024ax or by visiting https://us.dynabook.com and selecting "Recall Information" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15.5 million (In addition, about 1.3 million were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 679 reports of the recalled AC adapters overheating or catching on fire, melting, and burning. This includes 43 reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide and on Toshiba.com from April 2008 to April 2014, sold with laptops or separately priced between $25 and $75.

Importers.Name: Dynabook Americas Inc., of Irvine, California (importer at the time of sale was Toshiba America Information Systems, Inc.)

Hazards.Name: Overheating and sparking, leading to burn and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled adapters and contact Dynabook for a free replacement. To check if their adapter is affected, they can visit https://support.dynabook.com/axx2024. A photo of the adapter with the power cord cut must be submitted to dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com to receive a replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Dynabook recalls 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters due to fire hazards. Stop using them and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: CableMod is recalling its 12VHPWR Angled Adapters, specifically models V1.0 and V1.1, designed to connect to graphics processing units at 90-degree or 180-degree angles. The V1.1 model features a stabilizing pin on the male connector. These adapters are available in various colors including black, blue, gold, green, orange, pink, purple, red, silver, and white, with the brand name "CableMod" printed on the side. They come in two configurations: Variation A for 90-degree connections and Variation B for 180-degree connections, with specific dimensions for each model.
recalls.Title: CableMod GPU Angled Adapters Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach CableMod at 888-203-3605 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, email support@cablemod.com, or visit https://cablemod.com/adapterrecall/ for more details.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 272 reports of the adapters becoming loose, overheating and melting into the GPU, with at least $74,500 in property damage claims in the United States. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at small independent retailers nationwide and online at vprbrands.com from September 2022 to November 2023, priced around $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to overheating and melting of the adapter's male connector.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled adapters immediately and contact CableMod for safe removal instructions and a full refund, including shipping costs, or a $60 store credit for non-custom products, with free standard shipping.

recalls.metaDescription: CableMod recalls GPU Angled Adapters due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for refunds or store credit. Learn more here.

recalls.Description: Snap has issued a recall for the rechargeable lithium-ion battery that comes with the Pixy Flying Camera, a compact device designed for capturing photos and videos. The battery, which is sold separately, is approximately three inches long and half an inch wide, while the Pixy camera measures about five inches by four inches, both featuring a yellow color scheme. The recall is prompted by concerns that the battery may overheat, creating a potential fire hazard.
recalls.Title: Snap Recalls Pixy Flying Camera Lithium-Ion Battery Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Snap's recall hotline at 800-269-6990, available 24/7, or email support@pixy.com. Additional information can be found online at https://support.pixy.com/recall or by visiting www.pixy.com and selecting 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page. Snap is also directly notifying all known purchasers.

Images.Caption: Recalled battery for Pixy Flying Camera
Pixy Flying Camera with recalled battery on charger

Injuries.Name: Snap has received four reports of the battery overheating and bulging, resulting in one minor battery fire and one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Pixy.com and Amazon.com from May 2022 to December 2023, priced between $185 and $250 for the Pixy Flying Camera or 'Flight Pack' bundle, $40 to $50 for an extra charger/battery, and $16 to $20 for the battery sold separately.

Importers.Name: Snap LLC., of Santa Monica, California

Hazards.Name: Overheating battery posing a fire risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the Pixy Flying Camera, remove the battery, and cease charging it. Visit www.pixy.com for recall participation instructions, refund details, and battery disposal guidelines per local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Snap recalls Pixy Flying Camera lithium-ion battery due to fire hazard. Learn how to participate in the recall and get a refund.
